name: Exercise training modes in young and older humans
summary: A 12-week randomized human exercise-training study comparing high-intensity interval training, resistance training, and combined training in young and older adults, with a sedentary run-in/control component for the combined-training group.
population: Sedentary young adults aged 18 to 30 and older adults aged 65 to 80 without major cardiometabolic disease

trial_details.horizon_note: Results are already in the public evidence layer, so this is not an open no-result watch item; ClinicalTrials.gov had no posted registry results in the June 14, 2026 API view.
trial_details.why_it_matters: Results matter for Mitochondrial Biogenesis and Dynamics because they can show whether the intervention changes function, biomarkers, and mechanistic endpoints in Sedentary young adults aged 18 to 30 and older adults aged 65 to 80 without major cardiometabolic disease. That is still narrower than proof of broad aging reversal.
